key_points: Bholi, whose real name is Sulekha, suffers neglect due to her appearance and stammer
Her childhood accident and illness shape her fearful nature
School becomes the turning point in her life
The teacher plays a crucial role in building her confidence
Education enables Bholi to speak boldly and take decisions
The story criticises dowry and gender discrimination
characters: Bholi (Sulekha) – a neglected girl who transforms through education
Ramlal – a caring but socially conditioned father
The Teacher – a progressive and compassionate guide
Bishamber Nath – a symbol of greed and dowry system
important_events: Her fear on the first day of school
message: Girls must be given equal opportunities
exam_tips: Link the wedding scene to women empowerment
Use terms like confidence, dignity, transformation
